one_line_takeaway: KUDA localizes knowledge-storing FFN layers via causal tracing, unlearns by deviating their representations away from the original model (cosine-complement loss) under a relaxed null-space projection that protects retain-set directions, and on MUSE/WMDP benchmarks it matches or beats RMU (the paper's stated prior SOTA) in forgetting-utility trade-off, with the largest margin on Qwen3-8B where RMU's empirical layer selection reportedly transfers poorly.
key_finding: On MUSE-BOOKS, KUDA achieves VerbMem-Df 0.05, KnowMem-Df 26.73, PrivLeak 16.55, KRD 0.8792, KnowMem-Dr 61.97 (vs. Origin 99.59/58.76/56.68/0.0000/67.00 and Retrain 14.35/28.90/0.00/1.0000/74.38); on MUSE-NEWS, KUDA achieves VerbMem-Df 8.71, KnowMem-Df 0.26, PrivLeak 20.09, KRD 0.9523, KnowMem-Dr 53.08 (vs. Retrain 20.80/33.30/0.00/1.0000/54.68), and the paper states KUDA minimizes retention degradation on NEWS while maximizing removal, though it is slightly weaker than WHP on BOOKS retention. On WMDP with Zephyr-7B-Beta, KUDA reduces Acc-Bio/Acc-Cyber/Acc-Avg to 29.92/26.82/28.03 (KRD 0.8879, MMLU 57.14) versus Origin 63.82/43.78/51.60 (MMLU 58.13) and RMU 29.22/27.93/28.43 (KRD 0.8665, MMLU 56.90) -- the paper characterizes this as removing 88.8% of hazardous knowledge with only a 1.3% MMLU drop. On Llama-3.1-8B, KUDA reaches KRD 0.9350 / MMLU 60.64 versus RMU's KRD 0.8888 / MMLU 58.33 (Origin MMLU 63.31). On Qwen3-8B the gap is largest: KUDA reaches KRD 0.9360 / MMLU 68.92 versus RMU's KRD 0.6304 / MMLU 60.02 (Origin MMLU 73.01), which the paper attributes to RMU's empirical layer-selection generalizing poorly to Qwen-3's architectural changes (QK-Norm, deeper stack) while KUDA's causal-tracing-based selection transfers better. In the Retention Transferability experiment, KRD stayed above 0.9 across datasets while MMLU degraded only marginally: 58.13 to 55.50 (Zephyr), 36.81 to 36.23 (MUSE-NEWS origin), 26.52 to 25.97 (MUSE-BOOKS origin).
core_contribution: KUDA (Knowledge Unlearning by Deviating representAtion) is a representation-level LLM unlearning method with three coupled components, all presented as the paper's own contribution (not related work): (1) a component-level layer-selection procedure that uses causal tracing plus a newly defined 'causal effect' metric to identify FFN sublayers (as opposed to MHSA sublayers) specialized in knowledge storage, combined with a sliding-window search (Algorithm 1) that selects a contiguous block of unlearning layers satisfying a >0.5 Hit-Ratio condition; (2) a knowledge-representation-deviation objective that pushes the hidden representation of forgetting-set inputs away from the original model's representation via a cosine-complement (1-cos) loss inspired by preference optimization (Eq. 3), paired with an L2 retaining loss on retain-set representations (Eq. 4), summed without a scaling factor (Lu = Lf + Lr); and (3) a 'relaxation null-space projection' mechanism that computes the uncentered covariance of retain-set inputs to the FFN's last linear layer (Wout/Wdown), applies SVD, and projects unlearning gradients into an approximate (relaxed via threshold tau, not strict zero-singular-value) null space of that covariance before applying them, using a 'stochastic proportional sampling' trick to keep the covariance estimation tractable. The paper also proposes a two-stage hyperparameter tuning strategy (fix beta=0.1 to find a 'stability boundary' on tau, then sweep beta in {0.1,1.0,2.0}) that decouples the joint (beta, tau) search into two 1-D searches.
threat_model: This is not an attacker/defender security paper; there is no adversary model. The 'risk' being addressed is that LLMs pretrained on large web corpora memorize sensitive personal information, copyrighted content, and dangerous/hazardous knowledge (biosecurity and cybersecurity know-how, per the WMDP benchmark), and that existing alignment/refusal training is 'shallow' and can be circumvented by adversarial prompting, so the encoded knowledge itself should be removed from model parameters rather than merely suppressed at the output/refusal layer. KUDA targets a benign 'unlearning' setting: the model owner has an identified forget set (Df, e.g. Harry Potter text, BBC news post-cutoff, WMDP bio/cyber corpora) and a retain set (Dr, e.g. Wikitext, Fandom wiki) and wants a resulting model whose behavior approximates a model that was never trained on Df, while a separate 'retrain' model serves as the gold-standard reference where available.
limitations: The paper's own stated caveats and observable gaps: (1) no 'retrain' gold-standard model exists for WMDP, so forgetting quality there is judged only against baseline comparisons, not a true retrained-from-scratch reference; (2) causal tracing is computationally expensive, so it is run only once per model on a generic '1,000 factual statements' probe set rather than per unlearning task, which the authors acknowledge is a practicality trade-off rather than a fully task-specific localization; (3) on MUSE-BOOKS, KUDA is explicitly reported as slightly weaker than WHP in retaining utility, i.e., it does not dominate on every metric/dataset; (4) the null-space projection requires an approximate (sampled) covariance estimate via 'stochastic proportional sampling,' introducing a sampling-ratio hyperparameter and approximation error versus the full retain-set covariance; (5) the relaxation threshold tau trades off forgetting strength against utility retention and requires the proposed two-stage search procedure rather than being set analytically; (6) the method modifies only the last linear transformation (Wout/Wdown) of FFNs in a selected contiguous layer window, so its scope is architecturally tied to the FFN-as-associative-memory framing and may not directly generalize to architectures where that framing is weaker.

datasets_used: MUSE-BOOKS (Harry Potter series text as forget set; Harry Potter Fandom Wiki as retain set)
MUSE-NEWS (BBC News articles published after August 2023, randomly partitioned into forget/retain sets)
WMDP-Bio (biosecurity hazardous-knowledge questions, collected from PubMed-derived sources)
WMDP-Cyber (cybersecurity hazardous-knowledge questions, collected from GitHub-derived sources)
'1,000 factual statements' probe set (used once per model for causal tracing / layer selection)
WMDP-Augment (variant with a domain-adjacent but benign retain set)
quantitative_results: MUSE-NEWS KUDA: VerbMem-Df 8.71, KnowMem-Df 0.26, PrivLeak 20.09, KRD 0.9523, KnowMem-Dr 53.08 (Retrain: 20.80/33.30/0.00/1.0000/54.68)
WMDP Llama-3.1-8B KUDA: Acc-Avg 26.28, KRD 0.9350, MMLU 60.64 (Origin MMLU 63.31; RMU: Acc-Avg 27.73, KRD 0.8888, MMLU 58.33)
WMDP Qwen3-8B KUDA: Acc-Avg 27.05, KRD 0.9360, MMLU 68.92 (Origin MMLU 73.01; RMU: Acc-Avg 35.30, KRD 0.6304, MMLU 60.02)
Retention transferability: MMLU degradation 58.13 to 55.50 (Zephyr), 36.81 to 36.23 (MUSE-NEWS origin), 26.52 to 25.97 (MUSE-BOOKS origin), with KRD > 0.9 across all
baselines_compared: RMU (representation misdirection for unlearning; described as prior SOTA partial-parameter-update method)
